steps.title: Find the slope using the known data points
steps.content:
We have population data for two years: 52 thousand in 1993 and 219 thousand in 2013. Calculate the slope of the linear model:

$$m = \frac{219 - 52}{2013 - 1993} = \frac{167}{20} = 8.35 \text{ thousand per year}$$

steps.title: Apply the slope to find the 2017 population
steps.content:
From 2013 to 2017 is 4 years. Using the slope, the population increases by:

$$8.35 \times 4 = 33.4 \text{ thousand}$$

The population in 2017 is:

$$x = 219 + 33.4 = 252.4$$

Rounded to the nearest whole number: $x = 252$
